title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary: The reviewer wants three changes: (1) extract hard-coded condition type/reason strings (e.g., "NetworkPolicyReady") into typed constants, (2) clarify or fix the label/annotation merge strategy in `ensureNetworkPolicy` so the operator is explicitly authoritative rather than silently preserving stale fields, and (3) add a defensive runtime check for empty ingress sources in `buildKeystoneNetworkPolicy` to fail closed if CRD validation is bypassed.
external_feedback:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- The NetworkPolicy reconciliation logic uses hard-coded condition type/reason strings (e.g., "NetworkPolicyReady", "NetworkPolicyNotRequired") in multiple places; consider centralizing these as typed constants to avoid typos and make future refactors less error-prone.
- In ensureNetworkPolicy you overwrite the existing NetworkPolicy.Spec and merge labels/annotations only in one direction (desired -> existing), which means removed labels/annotations and any user-added spec fields will be silently preserved or dropped; if you want the operator to be truly authoritative, it may be worth explicitly documenting or adjusting this merge strategy so consumers understand which mutations are allowed to stick.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The NetworkPolicy reconciliation logic uses hard-coded condition type/reason strings (e.g., "NetworkPolicyReady", "NetworkPolicyNotRequired") in multiple places; consider centralizing these as typed constants to avoid typos and make future refactors less error-prone.
- In ensureNetworkPolicy you overwrite the existing NetworkPolicy.Spec and merge labels/annotations only in one direction (desired -> existing), which means removed labels/annotations and any user-added spec fields will be silently preserved or dropped; if you want the operator to be truly authoritative, it may be worth explicitly documenting or adjusting this merge strategy so consumers understand which mutations are allowed to stick.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path="operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_networkpolicy.go" line_range="70-79" />
<code_context>
+func buildKeystoneNetworkPolicy(keystone *keystonev1alpha1.Keystone) *networkingv1.NetworkPolicy {
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**🚨 issue (security):** Guard against empty ingress sources to avoid accidentally allowing all ingress if validation is bypassed.

Today CRD/XValidation requires `size(self.ingress) > 0`, but if validation is bypassed (old objects, disabled validation, direct etcd writes), `npSpec.Ingress` may be empty. In that case an `Ingress` rule with an empty `From` effectively allows all sources on port 5000, which is an unsafe default for a hardening feature. Please add a defensive check (e.g., return an error from `reconcileNetworkPolicy` or skip NetworkPolicy creation) when `len(npSpec.Ingress) == 0` so we fail closed rather than open.
</issue_to_address>
~~~

</details>

***

code_comments.body:
**🚨 issue (security):** Guard against empty ingress sources to avoid accidentally allowing all ingress if validation is bypassed.

Today CRD/XValidation requires `size(self.ingress) > 0`, but if validation is bypassed (old objects, disabled validation, direct etcd writes), `npSpec.Ingress` may be empty. In that case an `Ingress` rule with an empty `From` effectively allows all sources on port 5000, which is an unsafe default for a hardening feature. Please add a defensive check (e.g., return an error from `reconcileNetworkPolicy` or skip NetworkPolicy creation) when `len(npSpec.Ingress) == 0` so we fail closed rather than open.
code_comments.author: sourcery-ai[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added a defensive guard at reconcile_networkpolicy.go:56-63 that checks len(keystone.Spec.NetworkPolicy.Ingress) == 0 before building the NetworkPolicy and returns an error refusing to create a policy that would allow all ingress. A test (TestReconcileNetworkPolicy_EmptyIngress_ReturnsError at line 525) verifies the error is returned and no NetworkPolicy is created, ensuring fail-closed behavior when CRD validation is bypassed.

resolution_summary: All three review items were addressed in reconcile_networkpolicy.go and reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go. Hard-coded condition strings were extracted into typed constants (conditionTypeNetworkPolicyReady, conditionReasonNetworkPolicyReady, conditionReasonNetworkPolicyNotRequired) at lines 29-33 and used throughout. The ensureNetworkPolicy function received an explicit doc comment (lines 199-207) documenting the merge strategy: Spec is fully overwritten, labels/annotations are additively merged preserving user-added keys. The security issue was addressed with a defensive guard (lines 56-63) that returns an error when ingress sources are empty, failing closed rather than open, with a corresponding test added.
